name: Dilis
description: Small, schooling fish found near the coast, often used for fish paste and other local dishes. Also known as Bolinao or Tiki-Tiki.
location: Pangasinan coastline

name: Galunggong
description: A staple fish in Filipino diets, known locally as 'Galunggong,' and a significant catch in many parts of the Philippines, including the Ilocos Region.
location: Coastal waters of the Ilocos Region

name: Skipjack Tuna
description: An important pelagic tuna species commonly caught in the waters off Luzon, including the Ilocos coast.
location: Offshore waters of the Ilocos Region

name: Groupers
description: A group of high-value reef fish prized for their firm, white flesh. Found in coral reefs and rocky areas along the Ilocos coast.
location: Coral reefs and rocky areas off the Ilocos coast

name: Malaga
description: A popular rabbitfish in Ilocano cuisine, often prepared as kilawin (ceviche) or inabraw (fish soup). Common in seagrass beds and reef areas.
location: Ilocos Norte, Ilocos Sur

name: Maya-Maya
description: Commercially important reef fish with firm, flavorful flesh. Known locally as Maya-Maya and found in reefs off the Ilocos coast.
location: Coral reefs off the Ilocos coast

name: Cavalla
description: Active predatory fish often associated with reefs and coastal areas. Also known as Talakitok.
location: Coastal waters of the Ilocos Region

name: Yellowfin Tuna
description: A migratory pelagic tuna species highly valued for its meat. Also known as Bariles (juvenile).
location: Offshore waters accessible from the Ilocos Region

name: Tilapia
description: A widely farmed freshwater fish known for being a low-cost protein source in local communities.
location: Paoay Lake, La Union

name: Milkfish
description: The national fish of the Philippines, bangus is extensively farmed in brackish water fishponds and is known for its tender, flavorful meat.

name: Round Scad
description: A popular and affordable fish, galunggong is often fried and served in Filipino households.
location: coastal waters off Ilocos Norte and Ilocos Sur

name: Frigate Tuna
description: Known for its rich taste and is commonly used in various local dishes.
location: Caught in offshore waters near La Union and Pangasinan.

name: Sardines
description: Essential in local diets and often used in making dried fish or tuyo.
location: Frequently found in the waters off the coast of Ilocos Sur

name: Short Mackerel
description: Favored for its firm flesh and commonly grilled or fried.
location: Present in coastal regions, including the shores of Pangasinan

name: Spanish Mackerel
description: A sought-after fish for its flavorful meat, often used in kilawin.
location: Inhabits open seas but also caught near the coasts of La Union.

name: Redbelly Fusilier
description: Recognizable by its vibrant red color, this fusilier is commonly used in sweet and sour dishes.
location: Found in reef-associated areas, occasionally sold in Ilocos Sur markets.

name: Yellowstripe Scad
description: A small scad species that's affordable and commonly fried whole in local households.
location: Abundant in the coastal waters of Pangasinan.

name: Japanese Threadfin Bream
description: Known for its delicate flavor, often steamed or cooked in sour broth.
location: Caught in sandy bottoms off the coast of Ilocos Norte.

name: Bigeye Scad
description: Valued for its tasty flesh, a common catch among small-scale fishers.
location: Found in schools in coastal areas, including those near La Union.

name: Trevally
description: A strong swimmer and a favorite among sport fishers.
location: Inhabits both inshore and offshore waters near Ilocos Sur.

name: Ponyfish
description: A small, flat-bodied fish that's typically fried until crispy.
location: Common in shallow coastal waters around Ilocos Norte.

name: Pompano
description: Prized for its buttery texture, often grilled or baked whole.
location: Inhabits coastal and estuarine waters near Pangasinan.

name: Yellowfin Tuna
description: Known for its rich taste, often used in sashimi and grilled dishes.
location: Caught in open seas, sometimes landed in ports serving the Ilocos Region.

name: Starry Triggerfish
description: A type of triggerfish with tough skin, often used in stews.
location: Found in reef areas, occasionally available in Ilocos markets.

name: Bar-eyed Goby
description: Commonly found in freshwater and brackish environments.
location: Inhabits rivers and estuaries within the Ilocos Region.

name: Catfish
description: Known for its resilience and a common ingredient in adobo dishes.
location: Cultivated in fish farms and found in freshwater bodies in Ilocos.

name: Striped Snakehead
scientificName: Also called mudfish, it's valued for its firm meat and often grilled.
description: Found in rice fields, swamps, and rivers around the Ilocos Region.
location: Found in rice fields, swamps, and rivers around the Ilocos Region.

name: Silver Perch
description: A native freshwater fish prized for its delicate flavor.
location: Inhabits lakes and rivers, including those in and near Ilocos Norte.

name: Climbing Perch
description: Hardy and can survive in low-oxygen waters.
location: Found in ponds, swamps, and slow-moving waters in the Ilocos Region.

name: Sea Catfish
description: Often used in sour soups like sinigang.
location: Caught in brackish and coastal waters, including those near Pangasinan.

name: Ludong
description: A rare and expensive fish known for its unique taste.
location: Migrates through river systems, sometimes available in Ilocos markets.

name: Freshwater Sardine
description: The only freshwater sardine, endemic to the Philippines.
location: Primarily found in Taal Lake but occasionally sold in Ilocos Region markets.

name: Squid
description: Commonly used in various Filipino dishes.
location: Caught in coastal waters, including areas near the Ilocos Region.

name: Shrimp
description: Staple in many local recipes.
location: Farmed and wild-caught in and around the Ilocos Region.

name: Mud Crab
description: Known for their sweet meat, often steamed or cooked in coconut milk.
location: Found in mangroves and estuaries near the Ilocos Region.

name: Blue Swimmer Crab
description: Popular for their tender meat.
location: Caught in coastal areas, including those near Pangasinan.
